_doc: Pinned baselines for the graph-builder benchmark suite. Each entry records (a) the threshold encoded in the test name, (b) the wall-time / memory measured on the author's machine on the recorded date, and (c) the substrate parameters used. A future regression check can compare a fresh measurement against `measured_seconds` (or `measured_peak_mb`) and fail when drift exceeds a configurable percentage.

_authoring_machine._note: Windows nt._getfinalpathname dominates the cost on this machine; Linux / macOS dev machines typically run 3-5x faster. Threshold values are aspirational for cross-platform robustness.

test_graph_build_1000_files_under_3s._note: Single observed run on this machine: ~2.2 s build wall-time. Multi-trial median TBD when CI captures rolling history.

test_graph_build_10000_files_under_30s._note: Not run on the authoring machine — generation alone takes several minutes. Linear extrapolation from the 100-file and 1000-file points predicts ~22 s build wall-time on this Windows host. CI on Linux is expected to land near 5-10 s.

test_graph_memory_10000_files_under_500M._note: Not run on the authoring machine. Edge dataclasses at ~150 bytes each => ~30 K edges => ~5 MB attributable to edges. The 500 MB ceiling is dominated by frontmatter parse buffers and YAML safe_load intermediate state; expected peak ~50-100 MB on a fresh build.

test_graph_traversal_10000_files_BFS_und._note: Not run on the authoring machine. BFS with adjacency dict on 30 K edges is dominated by Python loop overhead, ~10 us per node visit; expected wall-time ~0.1-0.5 s on any modern machine.
